entry.title: Material Deletion Error
entry.content:

That particular material should not be allowed to use in the sales order at all.

For eg. Material ARF32101985

Please help.

CB User - CB9980000000

entry.title: Re: Alternative cost center for a wage type generated dur...
entry.content:
Enter a posting wage type in either IT14 (Recurring payment/Deduction) or IT15 (Additional Payments) within the employee's master record. 

In the payroll schema, populate the posting wage type amount with the value derived from the technical wage type (that one you mention in your question) with a PCR  

Best Regards

Giorgio

entry.title: Re: Does PRIMARY KEY clustered check stored somewhere
entry.content:
@chris_keating, apparently I need more help.

 

I tried to run the following code:

 

SQLLEN ind[1] = SQL_NTS;

int clusteredId = 0;

ret = SQLBindCol( m_hstmt, 2, SQL_C_SLONG, &clusteredId, 0, &ind1[1] );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, STMT_ERROR );
result = 1;
}

 

Apparently after executing SQLFetch() the ind[1] is still SQL_NTS despite the fact thar the field is NULL. It should have been SQL_NULL_DATA.

Did I pass wrong parameters in?

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Your guide to SAP Signavio at SAP Sapphire, Orlando 2026!
entry.content:
Calling all process owners and transformation leads!

The countdown to SAP Sapphire 2026 in Orlando has officially begun so we’ve put together a guide for you on everything SAP Signavio happening at the event this year.

In just a few weeks, we will be welcoming you back to Orlando for our annual three-day, in-person flagship event to discover how SAP helps organizations turn potential into performance, through value-driven transformation and process excellence. This event series will also spotlight SAP´s bold new AI vision and reimagined Joule experience.

This year, we will be part of the Business Transformation Management track again and will be presenting our integrated toolchain which encompasses SAP Signavio, SAP LeanIX and WalkMe, combined with Tricentis and Syniti. Over at SAP Signavio, we´ll be focusing on how you can bring direction to ERP transformation and how to deliver impact through unleashing process and AI excellence.

You‘ll find here an easy to navigate breakdown of the key SAP Signavio activities such as keynotes, sessions you don’t want to miss. You will also discover how to talk to our experts, what we´ll be doing at the Experience Center and how to join us virtually. Make sure you stay to the end to hear about some of the extra entertainment we have in store for you!

 

Keynotes

SAP:

- May 12, 8.30-10.00am – The Beginning of Better with Christian Klein SAP, Muhammad Alam SAP, Philipp Herzig SAP, and Sebastian Steinhaeuser SAP

 

 

Business Transformation Management:

- May 12, 10.30-10.50am - Build a transformation capability that delivers measurable value with Sam Masri SAP

 

 

SAP Signavio: 

- May 13, 2.00-2.20pm - Unlocking the New Frontier of Agentic Transformation with SAP Signavio with Dr. Gero Decker SAP

As companies deploy hundreds of AI agents, the real differentiator is no longer automation alone but instead how intelligence is captured and turned into value. Discover how SAP Signavio solutions maximize AI agent impact with the new frontier of process excellence. Learn how to transform collective operational intelligence into sustained impact.

 

Session Highlights

Business Transformation Management:

- May 11, 1.30-1.50pm - Built to Change: How Adaptable Enterprises Win with Oscar Ramos SAP and Jose Cabel SAP

- May 12, 11.30-11.50am - Value as the Common Currency for Transformation and Continuous Improvement with Myrna Acosta SAP

- May 13, 2.30-2.50pm - AI Native... or AI Naive? People and Operations in the Age of Agentic AI with Dee Houchen SAP and David Letteer Hensel Phelps Construction Co

 

SAP Signavio:

- May 12, 10.30-11.15am - Crafting the future: The SAP Signavio solutions 2026 road map with Andre Wenz SAP

- May 12, 1.00-1.20pm - Why high performers focus on process excellence with SAP Signavio solutions with Josèphe Blondaut SAP

- May 12, 1.30-1.50pm - Deliver measurable transformation value confidently with SAP Signavio with Jane Fu SAP and Prasanna Shroti Gilead Sciences Inc

- May 13, 1.30-1.50pm - Unlock AI agent value with SAP Signavio solutions with Noor Naqvi SAP

 

ASUG:

- May 12, 11.30-12.15pm - Why ERP transformations fail: The change management gap with Kara Beck Moog Inc

- May 13, 2.00-2.45pm - Next-gen process management: Process atoms and AI agents from SAP Signavio with Silvio Arcangeli SAP

 

Talk to our experts 🗣️

Join us for 1 on 1 sessions in our expert center, where you can meet our experts who will offer practical guidance helping you with all your seemingly unsolvable problems and questions. Also join us at our demo stations to experience hands-on demos of our latest solutions.

 

SAP Signavio Demo Booths:

- Live showcase: The integrated toolchain for ERP Transformation - BTM2318

- Manage, govern, and get more value from your agentic enterprise with SAP - BTM2319

- Digital twin of the organization: strategic visibility for agentic AI – BTM2320

 

Experience Center ✨

Don´t forget to drop by our interactive showcase of SAP’s latest technologies, industry innovations, and strategic priorities at the Experience Center. This interactive experience will include SAP Signavio solutions and is designed to inspire future innovation and to get you thinking about new possibilities for your business.

 

Don´t Miss Out! 🎾

Celebrity Keynote from Roger Federer: Find your winning edge - May 13, 3:30 p.m.–4:30 p.m.

Join legendary tennis champion and philanthropist Roger Federer for his insights into excelling under pressure. Federer shares how his career-long lessons in versatility, resilience, and strategic thinking can help leaders deliver results no matter the business area.

 

 

 

Party Time 🎉

Live at Disney’s Magic Kingdom® Park: Dave Mathews Band - May 13, 7:30 p.m.–12:00 a.m.

Finish off your Sapphire experience with an unforgettable visit to the iconic attractions, beloved characters and delicious food at Disney’s Magic Kingdom® Park, with a private concert with Dave Mathews Band all against the magical backdrop of Cinderella´s Castle. This is a night of celebration you definitely won’t want to miss out on, and the perfect ending to Sapphire 2026.

 

 

Virtual Sapphire 🖥️

Can´t make it to Orlando? Don´t worry, you can still join us at SAP Sapphire Virtual, May 12-13. The virtual experience will feature live keynotes, daily streaming from the show floor, and ample opportunities to dive deeper into our topics.

Register here.

 

See you there 👋

Sapphire 2026 will be here before you know it so start planning your experience today! If you have any questions about SAP Signavio´s sessions, activities and offerings, feel free to send me an email or contact Jane Fu, Director Product Marketing at SAP Signavio.

entry.title: Re: Pm Order TECO Resrtiction
entry.content: This is bit complicated requirement, which cannot be fully enforced using configuration alone because standard user status only controls whether TECO is allowed, not whether business conditions like open reservations, pending PR/PO, unconfirmed operations, open goods issues, permits, or notification status are actually complete. A simple configuration approach is to define two user statuses under a status profile: INIT (TECO not allowed under business process trans set as forbidden) and READY (TECO allowed or no restrictions), where users manually ensure all checks are completed before moving to READY. However, if automatic validation is required when the user clicks on TECO flag on work order, then enhancements are necessary, such as IWO10004 to validate open reservations or PR/POs or goods movement or operation user status CNF for all operations, BAdi "WORKORDER_UPDATE" to validate if inbound deliveries are open, and QQMA0014 to validate notification is completed, ensuring TECO is only possible when all business rules are satisfied; otherwise, the system will only rely on user discipline without technical enforcement.

entry.title: How to do data purge to a Repository DataBase thats to big
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a Database of the main repository, with a big amount of data, and in the last 6 months grown 110GB.
The table PMLATT is 75% of the database Volume with 410GB, representing 2.6 billions records.

Is there any procedure or action so we can do the purging (Data Purge) of this Database?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to do data purge to a Repository DataBase thats t...
entry.content:
Hello, 

If you are running a PD version older than v16.7 SP08, please review the resolution section of KBA 3480518 - How to clean up the repository and remove unused data.
Always back up the database before executing the script for safety considerations.

The script described in the article has been implemented in PD 16.7 SP08 and higher versions, so you don’t need to run it manually—it is automatically executed when needed at connection time.

Kind regards

entry.title: The New Look of Success: Navigating the 2026 Experience Updates
entry.content:
We are in the midst of a major evolution for the SuccessFactors experience. Targeting three main areas: the Latest Home Page, the Latest People Profile, and the New Edit/History UI, these changes are designed to make the system feel less like a technical database and more like the modern, intuitive apps we use every day. While these changes may feel daunting, it opens the doors to de-clutter processes and move towards a people-first experience. 

Let’s dive into what’s changing, what we’re excited about, and most importantly, how to prepare for the shift!

The Latest Home Page: Your New Command Center

With the changes coming to the home page, it will feel more than a static landing page, but rather a dynamic launchpad. It is cleaner and more concise, focusing on exactly what needs to be done now.


Some Key Latest Home Page Features:

  • Quick Actions: The time savers of the homepage. Common transactions can be made directly from the homepage in the new quick-actions header, while navigation to specific pages can be done at the click of a button from the quick-links section. There can be up to 14 custom actions and links activated at once, making the day-to-day transactions of a business feel instant.
  • Personal Customization :Employees can now feel a sense of ownership over their digital workspace by having the option to choose which quick actions or quick links they want to show on their home page. If an employee prefers a cleaner look, they can remove their name from the greeting message as well - a small touch to add a bigger, personal feel.
  • Needs Attention Section:All home page cards that require attention are displayed together in one section rather than being broken up, reducing the ‘scroll fatigue’ that came from locating a task notification. These cards can be filtered based by category and type, making navigation a breeze.
  • Organizational Updates: This section of the homepage will retain its standard functionality - providing teams with a space to post about company news & custom content. All of these can be grouped into separate tabs (folders), keeping the page concise while ensuring no important news is missed

Some items to keep in mind:

  • Personalization Guardrails: Quick actions can be made un-hideable by the system admin, ensuring employees don't accidentally hide essential HR tools they actually need
  • Layout Changes: Some buttons/items will now be found in different areas of the homepage. For example, the Team Absence Calendar has shifted from ‘Quick Actions’ over to ‘Quick Links.’
  • Branding Changes: While the traditional banner image has been removed in the latest experience, a new setting ‘Brand Image’ has been added - where the applied image appears on the greeting banner of the homepage
  • Reduced Navigation Option: Quick actions that were already in the main home page drop down (like the Org Chart) have been removed to reduce redundant navigation
  • Filtering of Tasks: The way filters work for tasks in the 'Needs Attention' section actually correspond to how cards were organized in the legacy experience.
  • Custom Tiles: Your old tiles might look a bit different in the new layout. It's a good time to check if the images still fit and if the text used is still relevant
  • Theming: The new home page uses more white space, so your corporate colors might need a slight tweak in your theme(s) to ensure everything flows and fits the companies branding style

Reminder - The Latest Home Page update is currently being turned on in preview environments by default - making now the perfect time to familiarize yourself with your customization options, and get a feel for the flow before it becomes the standard.

The Latest People Profile: Spotlight on Your People

With the update to the people profile, we are moving away from the long-page scrolling view to a sleek, card-based interface. The two distinct areas of updates that are brought in with this new experience are the Profile Preview & Spotlight and the Full Profile. 

 Some Key Spotlight Features:

  • Growth Portfolio Integration: A centralized view of an employee's skills, competencies, and attributes. If enabled, it brings the Talent Intelligence Hub directly into the profile
  • Career Development Integration: Highlights future-focused data like target roles and development goals. This will help target focus towards career growth for managers & their direct reports
  • Organization Information: A simplified header that provides instant context on where the person sits in the hierarchy, including their manager and location, without needing to click into the Org Chart
  • Badges: Employees can see their earned badges instantly, boosting engagement and peer-to-peer appreciation

Some Key Profile Preview Features

  • Quick Actions: Perform some common tasks directly from the spotlight without navigating away from the current page

Some Key Full Profile Features:

  • Categories & Cards: Data is now organized into logical 'Cards' (ex. Personal Information, Payment Information - referred to previously as Portlets) grouped under 'Categories'. This eliminates the infinite scroll layout and allows users to jump directly to the data they need
  • Centralized Permission Control: Admins can now control the visibility of categories & cards within through the 'Configure Latest People Profile' tool. By default, all categories are made visible to everyone or you can choose to specify the permission roles you want to grant access for. From there, the roles can be updated via the 'Permission Roles' tool for more updates, ensuring data privacy is maintained
  • Direct Reports & Team Size View: On a manager profile, you can now view the number of employees who directly report to the manager and the size of the manager's team. This feature is available to be displayed on the Full Profile header.
  • Profile Header Fields: You can now add up to three fields to Full Profile header, allowing you to display additional employee information
  • Actions & Links: Through the actions icon, users will have access to a list of actions & links divided into four groups: Change, View, Take Action, and Links

Some items to keep in mind:

  • Card Organization Blocks: Predefined cards are now locked to specific categories. For example - an employees Payment Information is locked to the Personal Information category of the profile
  • Permission Control: Some fields that were visible in the legacy view may require a quick RBP (Role-Based Permission) check to ensure they appear correctly in the new card-based layout
  • Block Descriptions: The feature to add a block description for custom cards has now been moved to ‘Help Text’, which can be used for all Full Profile cards

The New Edit & History UI: Precision and Transparency

Building on the Full Profile update, the New Edit/History UI replaces the old pop-up windows with streamlined experience. The focus here is on transparency, giving users a clear view of where a record has been and where it is going. This feature is available only for customers who are currently using or are planning to implement Employee Central.

Some Key Features

  • Field Masking: Previously, masked fields were often revealed during the edit process. In the new UI, sensitive values remain masked by default even while editing, ensuring privacy of employee data in all areas
  • Visual Change Tracking: With the previous edit UI, if a field was automatically updated due to onChange rules or associations, there was no clear indicator of this additional change. Now, the changed field will be highlighted in blue, with a pop-up message informing the user that the field was changed based on another field's change
  • Accessible Save Button: Previously, the Save button only enabled when a change was performed. This has been updated to be permanently active, with the system informing you that your record contains no modifications if true upon clicking
  • Consistent Experience: Whether you are updating a phone number, a dependent, or job information, the UI looks and behaves exactly the same way
  • Workflow Clarity: The New Edit UI is fully integrated with Workflows. If a change requires approval, the UI clearly indicates that the "Change is Pending Approval" directly on the card.

Some items to keep in mind:

  • Field Grouping & Logic: With the legacy Configure People Profile admin tool, you were able to organize how fields are displayed on the Editing UI of specific Employee Central blocks. With the new UI, fields will appear in the same order on the Edit UI as they do on the View mode of the portlet, which is based on the data model configuration (BCUI)
  • Field Masking: If you choose to unmask a field while making edits to a record, you won't be able to re-mask it during that specific editing session
  • Shift in Validation Logic: Error messages triggered by onChange rules will now be displayed as warning messages, allowing users to proceed on the UI. To trigger error messages that prevent saving, you will now need to have a distinct onSave rule applied

The Switch: How to Prepare

To ensure a smooth transition to these new experiences, here are a few items to review with your teams for alignment

  • Documentation: Update your internal "How-To" guides. Screenshots of the old pop-up windows will quickly look outdated once the new side-panel UI is enabled
  • Data Accuracy: Since the new UI has stricter validations, it might surface legacy data errors that the old UI ignored. Use this as an opportunity for a "data spring cleaning”
  • Audit Your Permissions (RBP): Since the new profile changes how data is displayed, double-check your Role-Based Permissions to ensure "Confidential" fields stay confidential in the new card views
  • Business Configuration (BCUI) Cleanup: Since the New Edit UI will now display fields following the BCUI layout, take some time to review your Data Model and ensure the most important fields are at the top of each element so users don't have to hunt for them in the new card layout
  • Validate Business Rules: Since onChange rules now behave as warnings in the new UI, now is the time to review your validations. If you have a "must-have" validation that should prevent a user from saving incorrect data, ensure you have a corresponding onSave rule
  • Mobile App Review: The Latest People Profile and Home Page are designed to be mobile-first. If your organization uses the SuccessFactors Mobile App, test these changes on a mobile device. Ensure that your custom quick actions and card layouts translate well to a smaller screen to provide a consistent experience across all platforms
  • Theme Manager Review: The new visual theme introduces more white space and specific card styling. Check your Theme Manager settings to ensure your company’s primary brand colors provide enough contrast against the new card backgrounds. You may find that buttons or links need a slight color tweak to remain accessible and stand out against the cleaner layout

While change can feel like a lot of moving parts, these updates are a large step towards a modern, intuitive HR ecosystem. With these changes, you'll be using a more empowered and efficient way of working within your SuccessFactors Environment.

entry.title: Sales Configuration display by CDS with table function/HANA SQL for Embedded Analytics and Fiori RAP
entry.content:
In that blog I want to share approach to display Sales Item Configuration in CDS-way. The approach could be used both in new reporting technology (like Fiori RAP-reporting (managed and unmanaged), Embedded Analytics) and in classical (ALV classic Dynpro, SE16N, SE16H etc.)

Technically, the approach is using the following:

  • Table Function with HANA SQL with scalar and table variable declaration
  • Functions STRING_AGG for row concatenation, CAST for casting to target type, CASE for conditional logic in HANA SQL
  • Window function for grouping and splitting the logic inside SQL-procedure
  • Sorting inside database procedure

So, it can be used both for Sales Item Configuration and as a cheat-sheet for HANA SQL in AMDP-classes.

The blog is dedicated to Natalya Yakimenko; really encouraging and supportive person especially for Analytical Tools in Big Data / HANA.

What is Sales Item Configuration?

In sales order we can parametrize item (configure) with characteristics according to customer needs: width, length, color, chemicals, mechanicals and so on. The configuration – is what the customer wants; so, it could be not only exact values, but interval values also; for example, Carbon (C) between 1 and 2 %. Also, it could be several values: for example, RED, YELLOW color.

On the Figure 1 it is shown sample of exact and multiple values. LENGTH has exact value and Colour has multiple values.

Figure 1 Exact value with UOM for LENGTH and multiple values for Colour

 

On the Figure 2 it is shown interval values in Sales Item Configuration. Carbon (C) has interval from 1 to 2 percents and Hydrogen (H) should be strictly more than 5% (also pay attention to different decimals part on the screenshot).

Figure 2 Sample of Interval values in Sales Item Configuration

 

How to read the configuration programmatically for reporting purposes?

Classical approach with function

In table VBAP (sales items) there is a special field (VBAP-CUOBJ, or CDS I_SalesDocumentItem-ProductConfiguration ) that links Sales Item Data Model to Variant Configuration Data Model.

For classical reporting very often the function VC_I_GET_CONFIGURATION_IBASE was used. It is still good function, and can be used, however

  • it does not allow us to select by characteristics values
  • it hardly can be used inside new approaches like Embedded Analytics and Fiori RAP-reporting
    DATA lt_CONFIGURATION     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF conf_out.
    DATA lT_CONF_WITH_AUTHOR  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF  api_value .

    CALL FUNCTION 'VC_I_GET_CONFIGURATION_IBASE'
      EXPORTING
        instance            = iv_cuobj                 
      TABLES
        configuration       = lt_CONFIGURATION                  
        et_conf_with_author = lT_CONF_WITH_AUTHOR               
      EXCEPTIONS
        instance_not_found  = 1
        OTHERS              = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4 INTO sy-msgli.
    ENDIF.

 

For Embedded Analytics and RAP-applications I decided to create two group of CDS (view entities):

  • Sales Configuration in row-mode with specifying master info about characteristics itself: data type, single/multiple etc.
  • CDS with table function which present any char in the one line.

 

CDS with values in each Row

The CDS (view entity) for Sales Configuration in row-mode with master data is the following. We could see that core tables are: IBIN, IBINVALUES, IBSYMBOL, CABN, CABNT.

The base part: 

define view entity ZI_CUOBJ_CLS300
  as select from ibin       as ibh
    inner join   ibinvalues as ibv    on ibh.in_recno = ibv.in_recno
    inner join   ibsymbol   as ibsymb on ibsymb.symbol_id = ibv.symbol_id
    inner join   cabn       as CABN   on  cabn.atinn = ibsymb.atinn
                                      and cabn.adzhl = '0000'


  association [1] to I_ClfnCharcDesc         as _CharDescr  on  $projection.CHARCINTERNALID   = _CharDescr.CharcInternalID
                                                            and _CharDescr.Language           = 'E'
                                                            and _CharDescr.TimeIntervalNumber = '0000'

  association [1] to ZI_CHARVALUE_MD as _CharValueH on  $projection.CHARCINTERNALID =  _CharValueH.CHARCINTERNALID
                                                            and $projection.CharcValuestr      =  _CharValueH.CHARCVALUE
                                                            and $projection.CharcValuestr      <> ''

{
  key ibh.instance                      as cuobj,
  key ibh.in_recno                      as IN_RECNO,
  key ibv.in_segmcnt                    as IN_SEGMCNT,

      ibsymb.atinn                      as CHARCINTERNALID,
      cabn.atnam                        as CharHure,
      _CharDescr.CharcDescription       as CharcDescription,
      cabn.atfor                        as dt_type,
      cabn.anzst                        as NumOfChars,
      cabn.anzdz                        as NumOfDecimals,
      cabn.msehi                        as char_uom,
      cabn.atein                        as Single_Mark,
      ibsymb.atcod                      as IntervalType,

      ibsymb.atwrt                      as CharcValuestr,
      ibv.dec_value_from                as CHARCFROMDECIMALVALUE,
      ibv.dec_value_to                  as CHARCUPTODECIMALVALUE,
      ibv.date_from                     as CharcFromDate,
      ibv.date_to                       as CharcUptoDate,
      ibv.time_from                     as CharcFromTime,
      ibv.time_to                       as CharcUptoTime,
      _CharValueH.CHARCVALUEDESCRIPTION as CHARCVALUEDESCRIPTION,


      /* associations */
      _CharValueH

}

 

Part: ZI_CHARVALUE_MD

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'MasterData for Char Value'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ZI CHARVALUE_MD
  as select from I_ClfnCharcValue as ValID
  association [0..1] to ZI_CharcValueDesc as _ValDesc     on  $projection.CHARCINTERNALID    = _ValDesc.CharcInternalID
                                                                  and ValID.CharcValuePositionNumber = _ValDesc.CharcValuePositionNumber
                                                                  and _ValDesc.Language              = 'E'
                                                                  and _ValDesc.TimeIntervalNumber    = '0000'

  association [1..1] to I_ClfnCharcBasic          as _ClasCharBsc on  $projection.CHARCINTERNALID     = _ClasCharBsc.CharcInternalID
                                                                  and _ClasCharBsc.TimeIntervalNumber = '0000'
{
  key ValID.CharcInternalID          as CHARCINTERNALID,
  key ValID.CharcValuePositionNumber as CharValuePos,
      _ClasCharBsc.Characteristic    as CharNamHuRe, --Human Readable
      ValID.CharcValue               as CHARCVALUE,
      _ValDesc.CharcValueDescription as CHARCVALUEDESCRIPTION,
      
      ValID.CharcFromDecimalValue    as CHARC_DECVAL,
      
      _ValDesc,
      _ClasCharBsc
}

 

part: ZI_CharcValueDesc

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Characteristcis Value Desc'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ZI_CharcValueDesc 
    as select from cawnt as CharacteristicValueDesc
  
      association [0..1] to I_Language as _Language 
        on $projection.Language = 'E'
             
{
  key   cast ( CharacteristicValueDesc.atinn as atinn_no_conv preserving type ) as CharcInternalID,
        @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: null
  key   CharacteristicValueDesc.atzhl as CharcValuePositionNumber,
        @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_Language'
        @Semantics.language: true
  key   CharacteristicValueDesc.spras as Language,
        @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: null
  key   CharacteristicValueDesc.adzhl as TimeIntervalNumber,
        @Semantics.text: true
        CharacteristicValueDesc.atwtb as CharcValueDescription,
        CharacteristicValueDesc.aennr as ChangeNumber,
        @Semantics.businessDate.from: true
        CharacteristicValueDesc.datuv as ValidityStartDate,
        @Semantics.businessDate.to: true
        CharacteristicValueDesc.datub as ValidityEndDate,
        CharacteristicValueDesc.lkenz as IsDeleted,
        
        _Language
}

 

The result we could see like that (from SE16H).

We could get values, uom (unit of measure), and data type description for each characteristic from sales item configuration. We could use it for searching and data validations (during migration for example) also.

 

Figure 3 Sales Item Configuration Sample

It is useful and informative, but it could lead to changes of cardinality in CDS-joins because we could have multiple values for some characteristics, also we need to do cast in each report/application and in case data type of characteristics is changed we need to change application. So, it would be better, for some cases, to get all values from one field. And it is possible with table function, as STRING_AGG is not working in CDS, but either in HANA SQL or ABAP.

 

Intervals could be like that (shown on figure)

 

Figure 4 Different types of intervals in Sales Item Configuration

 

 

CDS with table function / amdp-procedure on HANA SQL

In table function we need do the following:

  • Format values (especially numeric ones) to their format from master data
  • Concatenate values into one field
  • For intervals it is needed to put special symbols: prefix, delimeter and postfix.

 

As in rows-oriented CDS it is using the following tables: VBAP, IBIN, IBINVALUES, IBSYMBOL, CABN, CABNT.

The idea is simple:

  • Target output type is CHAR with length 120 (of course can be longer, but for my purposes it was more than enough). (pay attention to the field ATWRT_STR in the table function below)
  • All values are selecting and casting to the target type depending on decimals in the characteristic’s master data.
  • If there are multiple values – it is concatenated in rows by function STRING_AGG
  • And for output purposes it is concatenated not only with values itself but with special sign (less (<), less or equal (<=), more or equal (=>), more) (pay attention to table declaration _t_interval_extstr and filling) (see figure 2 with Hydrogen value)
  • The procedure returns sorted data – so it can be used in applications for binary search purposes without sorting on the application’s side

 

Code sample for table function for sales configuration’s output

@EndUserText.label: 'SOI - Conf Cls300 - AGG mode'
@ClientHandling.type: #CLIENT_INDEPENDENT

define table function ZI_SOI_CLS300_AGG
returns {
  slsh : vbeln_va;
  slsi : posnr_va;
  MATERIAL: matnr;
  Plant: werks_d;
  ItemDescr: arktx;
  PRODUCTCONFIGURATION: cuobj_va;
  
  ORDERQUANTITY: kwmeng;
  OrderQuantityUnit: vrkme;
  
  ATINN : atinn;
  atnam : atnam;
  ATWRT_STR: abap.char(120);
  }
implemented by method zcl_tf_soicls300_agg=>read_cls300_one_line;

 

Code sample for HANA SQL AMPD-procedure

CLASS zcl_tf_soicls300_agg DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-METHODS read_cls300_one_line
        FOR TABLE FUNCTION ZI_SOI_CLS300_AGG
      .

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_tf_soicls300_agg IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD read_cls300_one_line BY DATABASE FUNCTION
        FOR HDB
        LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
        OPTIONS READ-ONLY
        USING vbap
              ibin
              ibinvalues
              ibsymbol
              cabn
              .


    -- https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-sqlscript-reference/
    -- ... table-variable-type-definition?locale=en-us

        declare _t_interval_extstr table(
            atcod "$ABAP.type( ibvalcod )",
            pre_fix "$ABAP.type( char2 )",
            delim "$ABAP.type( char5 )",
            post_fix "$ABAP.type( char2 )"
        );

        declare _mandt varchar(3) := session_context('CLIENT');

* see data element  domanin: IBVALCOD
*0   Empty Quantity (No Value Allowed)
*1   = Lower limit
*2   [ Lower limit, upper limit )
*3   [ Lower limit, upper limit ]
*4   ( Lower limit, upper limit )
*5   ( Lower limit, upper limit ]
*6   ( - Infinite, upper limit )
*7   ( - Infinite , upper limit ]
*8   ( Lower limit, infinite )
*9   [ Lower limit, infinite )


        _t_interval_extstr =
            select '1' as atcod, '' as  pre_fix, '' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            union all
            select '2' as atcod, '' as  pre_fix, ' - ' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            union all
            select '3' as atcod, '' as  pre_fix, ' - ' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            union all
            select '4' as atcod, '' as  pre_fix, ' - ' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            union all
            select '5' as atcod, '' as  pre_fix, ' - ' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            union all
            select '6' as atcod, '' as  pre_fix, ' - ' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            union all
            select '7' as atcod, '' as  pre_fix, ' - ' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            union all
            select '8' as atcod, '>= ' as  pre_fix, ' - ' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            union all
            select '9' as atcod, '>= ' as  pre_fix, ' - ' as delim, '' as post_fix from dummy
            ;


          _single_n_intervals =
        with _low_fromto_agg as (

          select
           ibval.mandt,
           ibval.in_recno,
           ibsym.atinn,
           cabn.atnam,

           cast( cabn.atfor as char(70) ) as atwrt,

           case cabn.atfor
              when 'CHAR' then
                 cast(
                    string_agg( ibsym.atwrt, '; ' )
                    as char (120) )

              WHEN 'NUM' then


*    begin of num - case (it consists of two parts: with intervals and strict equal)

               case -- ibsym.atcod
*    no intervals
                when ibsym.atcod = '1' or ibsym.atcod = '8' or ibsym.atcod = '9' then

                 CASE cabn.anzdz

                    when '0' then
                         cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,0)) ),
                            ';'
                            )
                          as CHAR (120) )

                    WHEN '1' then

                         cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,1)) ),
                            ';'
                            )
                          as CHAR (120) )

                    WHEN '2' then

                         cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,2)) ),
                            ';'
                            )
                          as CHAR (120) )

                    WHEN '3' then

                         cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,3)) ),
                            ';'
                            )
                          as CHAR (120) )

                    WHEN '4' then

                         cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,4)) ),
                            ';'
                            )
                          as CHAR (120) )

                    WHEN '5' then

                         cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,5)) ),
                            ';'
                            )
                          as CHAR (120) )

                    WHEN '10' then
                         cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (21,10)) ),
                            ';'
                            )
                          as CHAR (120) )

                    ELSE
                         cast( string_agg( ibval.dec_value_from, '+ ' ) as char (120) )

                  END

              else
*    with intervals
                 CASE cabn.anzdz

                   when '0' then

                        cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat(
                                concat(
                                  concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,0)) ),
                                  interv.delim
                                  ),
                                concat( cast( ibval.dec_value_to as dec (18,0)), interv.post_fix )
                              )
                              , '; ' )
                            as CHAR (120) )

                   WHEN '1' then

                        cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat(
                                concat(
                                  concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,1)) ),
                                  interv.delim
                                  ),
                                concat( cast( ibval.dec_value_to as dec (18,1)), interv.post_fix )
                              )
                              , '; ' )
                            as CHAR (120) )

                   WHEN '2' then

                        cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat(
                                concat(
                                  concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,2)) ),
                                  interv.delim
                                  ),
                                concat( cast( ibval.dec_value_to as dec (18,2)), interv.post_fix )
                              )
                              , '; ' )
                            as CHAR (120) )

                    WHEN '3' then
                        cast(
                          string_agg(
                            concat(
                                concat(
                                  concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,3)) ),
                                  interv.delim
                                  ),
                                concat( cast( ibval.dec_value_to as dec (18,3)), interv.post_fix )
                              )
                              , '; ' )
                            as CHAR (120) )

                    WHEN '4' then
                            cast(
                              string_agg(
                                concat(
                                    concat(
                                      concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,4)) ),
                                      interv.delim
                                      ),
                                    concat( cast( ibval.dec_value_to as dec (18,4)), interv.post_fix )
                                  )
                                  , '; ' )
                                as CHAR (120) )

                     WHEN '5' then

                           cast(
                              string_agg(
                                concat(
                                    concat(
                                      concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (18,5)) ),
                                      interv.delim
                                      ),
                                    concat( cast( ibval.dec_value_to as dec (18,5)), interv.post_fix )
                                  )
                                  , '; ' )
                                as CHAR (120) )


                     WHEN '10' then
                           cast(
                              string_agg(
                                concat(
                                    concat(
                                      concat( interv.pre_fix, cast( ibval.dec_value_from as dec (21,10)) ),
                                      interv.delim
                                      ),
                                    concat( cast( ibval.dec_value_to as dec (21,10)), interv.post_fix )
                                  )
                                  , '; ' )
                                as CHAR (120) )


                     ELSE
                         cast( string_agg( ibval.dec_value_from, '+ ' ) as char (120) )
                     END

                 END


                END as atwrt_str


              from ibinvalues as ibval
                join ibsymbol as ibsym
             ON ibsym.symbol_id = ibval.symbol_id
             and ibsym.mandt = ibval.mandt

             join cabn as cabn
              on cabn.atinn = ibsym.atinn
              and cabn.adzhl = '0000'
              and cabn.mandt = ibsym.mandt


            join :_t_interval_extstr as interv
                on interv.atcod = ibsym.atcod


           where ibval.mandt = :_mandt

          group by ibval.mandt, ibval.in_recno, ibsym.atinn, ibsym.atcod, cabn.atnam, cabn.atfor, cabn.anzst, cabn.anzdz

        )

        select
          vbap.vbeln as slsh,
          vbap.posnr as slsi,
          vbap.matnr as material,
          vbap.werks as plant,
          vbap.arktx as itemdescr,
         vbap.cuobj as productconfiguration,

         vbap.kwmeng as orderquantity,
         vbap.vrkme as orderquantityunit,

         fromto_agg.atinn,
         fromto_agg.atnam,
         fromto_agg.atwrt_str

            from vbap as vbap

            inner join ibin       as ibh on vbap.cuobj = ibh.instance

            inner join _low_fromto_agg as fromto_agg on fromto_agg.in_recno = ibh.in_recno

            ;

        RETURN
            select * from :_single_n_intervals
            ORDER BY slsh, slsi, atinn
            ;

  eNDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 

As a result we could see different types of values in one cell but with different formats (with taken into account decimals, multiple values, and interval type)

Figure 5 Result Output of HANA SQL procedure

 

Conclusion

In that blog I have used HANA SQL for data selecting and formatting for Sales Configuration data model. Comments / additions / recommendations are welcomed both as about sales configuration and HANA SQL itself. Thank you.

For contacting with me: my linked.

HANA SQL help for aggregate functions could be found here.

entry.title: Re: Why Is My Content Marked as Spam?
entry.content:
It's not reasonable that if I finish it in a short time period it will be marked as SPAM.

Since it's very slow to load community blog editing page in Chinese Mainland (might be due to CN firewall issue?), our practice is that we'll prepare the content using HTML tag formatted offline and copy & paste the content to the Community blog editing page, and then do reviewing and minor changes after that (including a few format check and image pasting). So for Community, I as the user can do a new posting of long content in a very fast way.

Please consider this situation, especially for SAP employees' posting since we do stand for SAP to post officially insights.

entry.title: EWM REPL task into pick HU
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I have an issue with REPL tasks. The relationship is 1 WO → n tasks. When I need to confirm them in RF, I have to create a pick HU and put all products into the same pick HU. The system fills the destination HU with the pick HU for the first product, but from the second product onward, it does not allow me to enter the pick HU as the destination HU. This is a major problem for me because for the products that are not assigned to the pick HU, the system does not create the subsequent LOSC task.

Could anyone help me?
Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Key figure / column based security in Datasphere
entry.content:
Intro

Datasphere do not directly support Key figure / column based security. SAP suggests creating separate models or separate tables for key figures and use DAC or separate spaces to protect the key figures which is a lot of extra work and a lot of extra maintenance. We will give an idea to how to solve the problem a bit more dynamic.

Challenge

We have in principle 3 levels of information about product cost.  All can see level A, fewer can see level B and even fever level C.  The method we will show use some of the ideas from the general Data Access Control (DAC) to hide secrets, but with our own implementation.

In short:

  • Create a Column DAC table in your permissions / DAC space that contains information about what a user is allowed to see.
  • The information from the Column DAC table is joined into relevant fact views.
  • Create calculated measures that will be blank (or zero) if the user is not allowed to see them.
  • Remove the original "secret" measures from the model.

The details:

Like for standard DAC create a table in a separate space where only trusted people can maintain the data.

Highest_Cost_Type_Permitted can have default value A. Fill the table with user IDs and their highest cost type permitted.

Create a helper table CostFactors.

Fill the table with values 1 for visible and 0 / null for not visible

The factor fields can be used to multiply like C_COST = C_Factor * <original cost field> which can give a null value. The priority factors can be used to create a measure like Authorized cost = “A_Priority_Factor” * < original A_cost_value> + “BA_Priority_Factor” * < original B_cost_value> + “C_Priority_Factor” * < original C_cost_value> which will give a not null value.

Now create a view VR_User_Cost_DAC something like

SELECT
  1 AS "join_column",
  "FilterUser_0"."UserID" AS "UserID",
  "TR_CostFactors_1"."Highest_Cost_Type_Permitted" AS "Highest_Cost_Type_Permitted",
  "TR_CostFactors_1"."A_Factor" AS "A_Factor",
  "TR_CostFactors_1"."B_Factor" AS "B_Factor",
  "TR_CostFactors_1"."C_Factor" AS "C_Factor",
  "TR_CostFactors_1"."A_Priority_Factor" AS "A_Priority_Factor",
  "TR_CostFactors_1"."B_Priority_Factor" AS "B_Priority_Factor",  
  "TR_CostFactors_1"."C_Priority_Factor" AS "C_Priority_Factor"
FROM ((SELECT "TR_CostPermission_2"."UserID",
              "TR_CostPermission_2"."Highest_Cost_Type_Permitted"
       FROM "TR_CostPermission" AS "TR_CostPermission_2"
       WHERE upper("TR_CostPermission_2"."UserID") = session_context('APPLICATIONUSER')) AS "FilterUser_0" 
	 INNER JOIN 
	 "TR_CostFactors_ZZ" AS "TR_CostFactors_1" ON "FilterUser_0"."Highest_Cost_Type_Permitted" = "TR_CostFactors_1"."Highest_Cost_Type_Permitted");

in words find the record for the current user and join to the factor table to get the relevant factors (the SQL was generated from a graphical view...). The only special feature here is the use of session_context('APPLICATIONUSER') to get the current user.

In use

A fact view containing B or C types of measures must be modified. The example here has a driving table "TR_Salesorder_items":

Step 1 (fx), create artificial “join_column” type integer value 1

Step 2 / 3, Left Join to VR_User_Cost_DAC shared from the permission space

Join on “join_column” which is just an artificial column with the value 1

Step 4) (fx) Create calculated measures like:

C_Cost = C_Factor * <original C_cost_value>

Authorized_cost = “A_Priority_Factor” * < original A_cost_value> + “BA_Priority_Factor” * < original B_cost_value> + “C_Priority_Factor” * < original C_cost_value>

Step 5) Hide original cost measures.

Step 6) Maintain Semantic type on the newly created measures.

Now  B_Cost, C_cost and Authorized_cost will be safe to use.

In the post, you have mentioned the App ID: F8638, so I assume you are using the app 'Explore Migration Objects'.
As stated in SAP Help Portal - Explore Migration Objects, "This app is only relevant for the migration approach Migrate Data Using Staging Tables. Could you please double check the help documentation and test again?

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content:
=> However, I wonder - how come the initial install went thru without any issues?
The 'Snap::VAlidate' issue is linked to Linux compiler version. It started appearing after Linux OS patch level / minor version upgrade. 
So, you would have run with a different (lower) OS patch level when it worked.

=> Express Edition
This was discontinued, I believe. There is this trial option :
https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/sybase-ase/trial.html?url_id=text-us-sapcom-ase-trial-software

Bart

entry.title: CxO Payment Approval UX in My Inbox for Payment Requisitions - China
entry.content:
Why CxO’s Experience in Payment Approvals matters in Cloud ERP

In many Cloud ERP implementations, payment approval workflows are designed to “work”—but not necessarily to delight. That distinction matters more than most teams realize, especially when the final approvers are C‑level executives.

During a recent SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition deployment in China, a critical usability gap surfaced in the payment requisition approval experience for CxOs. The issue wasn’t functionality. It was experience.

And experience, at the executive level, determines adoption, cycle time, and ultimately trust in the system.

This is exactly why the new SAP Influence idea
“CxO Payment Approval UX in My Inbox for Payment Requisitions - China”
(described in https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/372233/?section=sectionDetails ) is so critical.

The Executive Reality: Approvals Happen in Between Everything Else

For CxOs—CFOs, CEOs, COOs, and senior finance leaders—payment approvals are rarely a primary task. They happen:

  • Between meetings
  • On mobile phones or tablets
  • Under time pressure
  • With legal and governance responsibility

In this context, every extra click matters.

Yet in the standard “My Inbox for Payment Requisitions – China” app, executives are required to navigate across six separate tabs just to understand one approval:

  • General Information
  • Requested Items
  • Payment Strategy
  • Attachments
  • Approval Details
  • Administrative Data

Critical business context—purchase orders, costs, margins, and original documents—are fragmented across the interface. The result Cognitive overload at exactly the moment when clarity is essential.

Where the Current Experience Falls Short

From an executive’s perspective, several pain points quickly emerge:

  1. No Information at a Glance

There is no single screen that answers the core executive questions:

  • What am I approving?
  • Why does it cost this much?
  • Is the documentation complete?
  • Who has approved this already?

The need to jump between tabs breaks decision flow.

  1. Attachments Are Out of Sight

Invoices, contracts, and supporting documents—often legally required—sit behind a separate tab. Executives can’t even tell at a glance whether documents exist.

  1. Missing Business-Critical Fields

Key data such as:

  • Purchase Order reference
  • Material or service details
  • Unit price and total amount
  • Gross margin

are either hidden or unavailable, forcing blind approvals or unnecessary rejection loops.

  1. Approval Actions Are Easy to Miss

Approve and Reject buttons are not consistently visible, especially on mobile devices, adding friction to the simplest part of the task: making the decision.

Rethinking Payment Approval Through a CxO Lens

The proposed redesign flips the paradigm from transaction-first to decision-first.

The guiding principle is simple:

Give executives everything they need to decide—on one screen, on any device, in seconds.

A Single, Scrollable Approval Page

All relevant information—financial data, line items, attachments, and approval history—appears on one consolidated page. No tabs. No hunting.

Business Context Front and Center

Key fields like Purchase Order number, material description, unit price, total amount (tax inclusive), and gross margin are surfaced immediately, enabling informed decisions.

Inline Attachments

Original business documents are visible directly within the approval screen. Executives can verify compliance without leaving context.

Clear Workflow Visibility

Approval steps and previous approvers are displayed inline alongside the business data, reinforcing accountability and governance.

Mobile-First by Design

The layout adapts naturally to phones and tablets, allowing approvals to happen wherever executives are—without compromising clarity.

Why CxO UX Is Not Optional in Cloud ERP

Improving executive UX isn’t a “nice-to-have.” It’s strategic.

Faster Cash Flow

Approval delays directly impact supplier payments and working capital. Removing friction accelerates decision-making.

Higher Adoption

If executives dislike the system, they will bypass it. That behavior quickly cascades through the organization.

Stronger Governance

Executives are legally and fiduciary responsible for approvals. Clear, complete information protects both the individual and the organization.

Better Customer References

Satisfied executives become champions—inside the company and in the market. Frustrated ones become blockers.

A Bigger Lesson for ERP Design

This case highlights a broader truth about Cloud ERP:

The most important users are often the least frequent users.

Designing for CxOs forces teams to prioritize:

  • Clarity over completeness
  • Insight over data volume
  • Decisions over transactions

When an approval experience works for a CEO on a mobile phone, it will work for everyone else too.

Final Thoughts

Payment approval is where finance, compliance, and executive accountability intersect. If the experience fails at this point, the entire ERP transformation is at risk.

By reimagining the payment approval UX around CxO needs, organizations don’t just improve a screen—they unlock faster operations, stronger governance, and greater confidence in their Cloud ERP investment.

Because when executives say “This system works for me,” the transformation truly succeeds.

Hi, 
The scenario described it is impossible to achieve it using BIB. BIB is intended to create employees at EC level and replicate them which implies there are several mandatory Infotypes. This are the mandatory ITs as per BIB integration replication guide:
Mandatory SAP ERP Infotypes:

  • IT0000

  • IT0001

  • IT0002

This implies not only you will have to replicate IT0001 but also IT0000. If just 3 Infotypes are going to be mapped a custom iflow or either export from EC and IDOC upload would be better and once employee replication is needed configure it to desire ITs as well as manadory ones. 
This URL contain that info

entry.title: Suppress Standard Address in Adobe Invoice Form – S/4HANA PublicCloud
entry.content:
We are working on an invoice output requirement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) where the business needs to display a different company address on the Adobe invoice form based on a specific condition (Profit Center B0011).

To achieve this, we have:

  • Created a custom field at the billing document header level
  • Implemented custom logic (BAdI) to populate the field based on Profit Center
  • Maintained Output Parameter Determination (OPD) to trigger a custom form template
  • Modified the Adobe form to display the required (new) address

With this approach, we are successfully able to populate and display the new address.

Issue:
The standard company code address is still being rendered by the system along with the new address, resulting in an overlap on the invoice output.

We have attempted to control visibility within the Adobe form; however, the standard address block still appears, likely due to standard binding from the data source.

Query:
Is there a recommended approach in S/4HANA Cloud to suppress or override the standard company address in the billing document Adobe form so that only the custom address is displayed?

Any guidance on handling or controlling the standard address node/subform would be helpful.

entry.title: Re: PTP replication in Employee central payroll
entry.content:
Hi Barath, 
For PTP to know the mapping you have to use the guide. Then in the guide you have the fields mapping as mentioned already in the discussion. This will include which field of EC would be mapped to what field on EC. 
Then at ECP configuration level there is a logic of how to map it. This is at SPRO path 
Personnel Management-->Integration Settings for SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll-->Configuration of Point-to-Point Replication-->For Point-to-Point Replication. This are the options for mapping:
- I Identical mapping
- S Mapping via SAP table
- C Mapping via customer table
- SI Mapping via SAP table or identical mapping
- CI Mapping via customer table or identical mapping
Mainly there are 2 tables one sap one custom to map values coming from EC to ECP or identical value. 
Then to increase PTP functionality, you have to go to extensibility part of the guide and there are several options depending the scenario. If it is an included field, infotype or not. From configuration extensibility to BADI one. 
If you were able to implement BIB, PTP I would say it is simpler and also you have this report to check the replication RP_HRSFEC_REPLICATION_STATUS for PTP master data. 
Regards,

entry.title: Re: Run Datasphere Task Chains from SAC Multi-Actions via External API Step
entry.content:
Hi @ambarish_kmd06580

 A 404 error usually indicates system can not find the task chain or task chain log you've specified or the tech user defined in oauth client does not has the privilege. It seems like a configuration issue. Could you please try sending below requests to ensure your task chain public API works well in DSP with the oauth client you specified? You may send 3 requests in sequence:

1. Get the access token of the oAuth client you specified in DSP, it should return the access token you need to use in next requests.
curl --request POST \
  --url https://<your dsp token url> \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data grant_type=client_credentials \
  --data 'client_id=<your oauth client id>' \
  --data 'client_secret=<your oauth client secret>'

 

2. Run the task chain and it should return {logId: xxxxxx}
curl --request POST \
  --url https://<your dsp host>/api/v1/datasphere/tasks/chains/<your DSP space>/run/<task chain id> \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer <the access token your get from the response of first step>'

 

3. Run the query to get the status of the task chain run
curl --request GET \
  --url https://<your dsp host>/api/v1/datasphere/tasks/logs/<your DSP space>/<the log id you get from previous step> \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer <the access token your get from the response of first step>'

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: G/L does not exist in company Code during Automatic P...

Hi

 

The account determination replaces the account symbol BANK with the G/L account number of the house bank account defined in the house bank master data. The appropriate masking can also be used for the bank subaccounts. in your scenario below will be masking 

  1. Main Bank Account- ++++++++++ 
  2. Incoming G/L Account-+++++1++++
  3. Outgoing G/L Account- +++++2++++

+ replaced by GL account in House Bank Account 

Check in FBZP House Bank details.  Your GL should be 10200012

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2604 – What’s New?
entry.content:
We’re excited to share the latest innovations across SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace delivered with the April 2026 release. This update focuses on enabling scalable carrier collaboration, improving operational control at docks and gates, and extending flexibility in modelling and visualizing tracking data.

Highlights at a glance:

 In addition to our functional updates, we are happy to share two new customer success stories with you in this edition, so make sure to read to the end.

 

Cross SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Self‑service testing enabled for carriers and data contributors

Carriers and data contributors can now use the Test Service functionality in test tenants to independently establish a connection with a test shipper and perform self‑service testing of their outbound integrations.

An internal shipper tenant is used to trigger the payload, with the shipper LBN ID and order ID predefined by SAP Business Network for Logistics. Two testing options are available:

  • With Default Payload – a static payload designed to validate connectivity from SAP Business Network for Logistics to the carrier system
  • With Custom Payload – allows carriers to modify order details to test different business scenarios

This enhancement reduces dependency on shipper coordination and accelerates onboarding and testing for carriers and data contributors registered after May 2024.

Scaling carrier collaboration: New partner service from Coneksion

To further support onboarding at scale, a new partner service from Coneksion is now available. The service leverages pre‑boarded carrier connection layers, simplifying connectivity and reducing integration effort when collaborating with large carrier networks.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Freight contracting EDI supports standalone products

Freight contracting EDI has been enhanced to support standalone products. With this release IFTMIN (UN/EDIFACT) can now map product item descriptions to FTX segments

The EDI documentation has been updated accordingly

Dock appointment scheduling: Granular role‑based access control

New, more granular roles are now available for Dock Appointment Scheduling and Gate Operations:

  • ManageDockAppointmentsDisplayTemplate
  • ManageDockAppointmentsProcessTemplate
  • ManageGateOperationsDisplayTemplate
  • ManageGateOperationsProcessTemplate

These roles are offered as an alternative to the existing roles and allow customers to restrict access exclusively to the ‘Manage Dock Appointments’ and ‘Manage Gate Operations’ apps. While existing roles remain fully supported, the new roles help enforce the principle of least privilege by limiting editing and navigation rights more precisely.

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Check-In on Hold now processed as an event

To improve execution transparency, Check‑In on Hold is now processed as a distinct event in the ‘Manage Freight Execution’ app.

Dock appointment scheduling: New fields for truck and trailer ID

To reduce manual entry and improve data consistency, users can now separately capture:

  • Truck ID
  • Trailer ID
  • License plate and country/region of registration

When truck and trailer IDs are entered in the ‘Freight Orders for Confirmation’ app, the data is synchronized with ‘Manage Dock Appointments’ and ‘Manage Gate Operations’, provided that no changes have already been made.

The new fields are available in the ‘Resource Details’ section, and the license plate field has been moved from ‘Driver Details’ to ‘Resource Details’.

In addition, the Consumer API for Dock Appointment Scheduling (GenericAppointment_Out) and the Consumer API to Manage Gate Operations (GenericGateProcess_Out) have been enhanced accordingly.

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Visibility control now also available for standard model fields

In the ‘Manage Models’ app, customers can now control the visibility of standard model fields, in addition to custom fields.

If the Visible checkbox is not selected, the corresponding standard model field is hidden from:

  • Filters and worklist settings
  • The Information tab of detail pages in fulfillment tracking apps

This enables cleaner, role‑specific UIs aligned with individual business requirements.

User‑defined tables can be added to tracking unit detail pages

Modelling flexibility has been further enhanced. Customers can now define Composition and Association‑to‑Many field types in the ‘Manage Models’ app and display them as user‑defined tables in the tracking unit detail page.

 

Previously, only plain user‑defined fields could be displayed. With this enhancement, more complex and structured datasets can be visualized directly in the tracking UI.

Improved data consistency with SAP TM in SAP S/4HANA

Data synchronization between SAP Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA and Global Track and Trace has been refined. Only the last correlated planned event is now synchronized, preventing inconsistencies and ensuring cleaner event timelines.

Shipment applications become more user‑friendly

Several usability enhancements have been introduced:

  • Standard Point Location Code (SPLC) is now displayed in location popovers across all fulfillment tracking apps
  • In the ‘Track Shipments’ app, search is now supported based on Carrier Reference Document ID

These enhancements make it easier to identify shipments and locations, especially in high‑volume scenarios.

 

Celebrating our customers’ success

On top of this month’s release highlights, we are proud to highlight two customer success stories that demonstrate the value of SAP Business Network for Logistics across different industries and transformation journeys.

Sasol, a global integrated energy and chemicals company, was recognized last week as a 2026 Innovation Award Winner for reimagining its logistics operations through intelligent transport optimization and real‑time freight collaboration. By implementing SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management together with SAP Business Network for Logistics, Sasol transformed a largely 3PL‑operated model into an in‑house, digitally connected transportation capability. This approach enabled greater flexibility, improved transportation planning and scheduling, and tighter alignment with Sasol’s business strategy focused on service performance and customer satisfaction.

View their pitch deck for more details.

In addition, we are excited to share a newly agreed reference story with Salling Group A/S, one of Europe’s largest retailers. Salling Group set out to replace manual, email‑driven coordination and fragmented logistics processes with an integrated, data‑driven approach. By leveraging SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace, the retailer gained clear, connected, and comprehensive visibility across its supply chain—from purchase to store—while simplifying collaboration with carriers and improving control over execution.

Go to their case study on sap.com

These customer stories highlight how SAP Business Network for Logistics helps organizations across industries increase transparency, strengthen collaboration, and build more resilient supply chains.

 

------------------------------------------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2604. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2604 release can be found under the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

My name is Sandra Solís, an ABAP Developer with a focus on ABAP, ABAP Cloud, and modern SAP development practices. I’m passionate about building clean, efficient solutions and continuously learning new technologies within the SAP ecosystem.

From my experience in SAP projects, learning sessions, and community events, the most valuable moments are always the same: Asking questions — any kind of question — and getting honest, practical answers from people who have been there.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Payment - DMEE File
entry.content:
Hi

When transferring data to a bank whether through a direct integration or a manual portal upload it is important to distinguish between two key dates:

File Header (Submission Date): This is the date the file or data packet is shared with the bank. It represents when the bank receives the instruction.

Line Item (Value Date): This is the specific date assigned to an individual payment record. The Value Date tells the bank exactly when to execute the transfer.

How it works: Banks can receive and store these instructions ahead of time. If the Value Date is in the future, the bank will hold the payment in a "pending" or "queued" status and only process the settlement once that specific date is reached.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 Release: Streamlining Data after Employment Terminations in SuccessFactors Employee Cent
entry.content:
Hi @almamontejano10,

The new setting is available for all event reasons, both existing and newly created ones.

It is delivered in a downward‑compatible way, meaning the default value of the setting is disabled. Only when this option is explicitly enabled within an event reason configuration, records will be automatically be deleted when they have that event reason and when the start date of that record is after the Termination Date. The deletion runs as part of the terminate process.
This ensures full control and no impact on existing customer configurations unless the setting is intentionally activated.

entry.title: Customer Engagement (IS‑U): “Transaction” menu greyed out for Installment Plans
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working as an SAP Consultant on Customer Engagement (CE) with SAP IS‑U.

In Customer Engagement, we have Installment Plans available in the left‑hand navigation panel. When expanding this menu, we can see options such as:

  • Show Installment Plans
  • Edit Installment Plan
  • Installment Plan History

When we select one of these options, the system correctly navigates to the Installment Plan view, where we can see:

  • Installment plan details
  • Master data
  • Installment plan history

The Business Partner is correctly confirmed, and the installment plan data is displayed without issues.

However, in the top‑right area of the screen, there is a “Transaction” button/menu. This menu contains several actions, such as:

  • Show Installment Plan
  • Edit Installment Plan
  • Show Payment plan/list
  • Edit Payment plan/list
  • Show Installment Plan Schedule
  • Edit Installment Plan Schedule

All of these transaction options are greyed out and cannot be selected.

There are no error messages shown. The installment plan can be displayed, but none of the transactions in the “Transaction” menu can be executed.

What is the root cause for the “Transaction” menu being greyed out in the Installment Plan view in Customer Engagement with SAP IS‑U?

Specifically:

  • Which customizing settings or parameters are typically missing or not correctly set?
  • Is this related to Transaction Launcher configuration, logical system / WebGUI setup, business role or navigation profile assignment, or installment plan status / locks?
  • Which SPRO customizing paths should be checked to enable these transaction options?

Any guidance on the correct setup or common pitfalls for this scenario would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.
